zone_6b_notes: More reliable than sweet cherry in Zone 6b â€” tart cherries handle Missouri winters and spring frost better. Montmorency is the gold standard tart cherry for home orchards in Missouri; self-fruitful, so no pollinator required. Plant on a slope or elevated ground to avoid frost pockets during April bloom. Birds will clean a tree in hours â€” netting as fruit colors is essential. Trees are smaller than sweet cherry (12â€“15 ft) and easier to manage. Montmorency typically yields 10â€“15 quarts per year at maturity. Harvest in June when fruit is deep red and slightly soft â€” juice is bright pink and flavorful.

planting.best_planting_time: Plant dormant bare-root trees March to early April

water: 1â€“1.5 inches/week during establishment and fruit development
